title: Coaching note — Session debrief 8 June 2024
summary: Coaching note — Session debrief 8 June 2024 Had a great session with the U16s this evening. Focused on the lineout after last week's issues at the maul. Key things I noticed: - The jumpers are not communicating the call clearly enough — the hooker is throwing before the jumper is set - Front lifter keeps coming up too early which is causing the jump to be off-balance - The call needs to come from the number 8, not the 4 or 5 What worked well: the walking lineout drills we tried at the start.
takeaway: Coaching note — Session debrief 8 June 2024 Had a great session with the U16s this evening.

title: Defensive Line Speed: How Elite Teams Are Winning the Gainline Battle
summary: Defensive Line Speed: How Elite Teams Are Winning the Gainline Battle Published: 10 May 2024 Source: The Rugby Coach Weekly In the modern game, defensive line speed has become the defining battleground at club and international level. Teams like Leinster, Toulouse, and the All Blacks consistently win the gainline battle not through brute force but through coordinated defensive systems that apply pressure before ball carriers can shift the point of attack. The blitz defence, once considered a high-risk strategy employed only at the elite level, is now being adapted successfully at club level.
takeaway: Defensive Line Speed: How Elite Teams Are Winning the Gainline Battle Published: 10 May 2024 Source: The Rugby Coach Weekly In the modern game, defensive line speed has become the defining battleground at club and international level.

title: World Rugby Law Amendment — Tackle Height Directive
summary: World Rugby Law Amendment — Tackle Height Directive Effective: 1 January 2024 SUMMARY FOR COACHES AND REFEREES World Rugby has implemented a new directive on tackle height across all levels of the game. The directive lowers the legal tackle height and introduces clearer guidance on what constitutes dangerous play. TACKLE HEIGHT The legal tackle height is now defined as at or below the line of the hips.
takeaway: World Rugby Law Amendment — Tackle Height Directive Effective: 1 January 2024 SUMMARY FOR COACHES AND REFEREES World Rugby has implemented a new directive on tackle height across all levels of the game.

title: Drill: Jackalling Circuit
summary: Drill: Jackalling Circuit Category: Breakdown / Contact Skills Duration: 20 minutes Age Group: Senior / U18 Equipment: 4 tackle bags, 4 shields, 8 cones, 1 ball per group OBJECTIVE Develop legal jackalling technique — arriving at the breakdown low and early with a stable body position to contest the ball. Set four breakdown stations (cones marking rucks) spaced around the perimeter. Place a tackle bag next to each station.
takeaway: EXECUTION Phase 1 — Technique Walk-Through (5 min) Coach demonstrates three jackal entry positions:  1.
